mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier financial conditions, lower discount rates, and higher equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive when policy is loosening rather than tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline during periods when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 3 consecutive months, or if the sign of the return effect is negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 6-month trend while FEDFUNDS has not risen enough to offset it, real rates fall and equities reprice higher over the next week on easier-than-expected policy conditions.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive 5-day SPX returns more often than untriggered observations, or if positive returns are absent when inflation exceeds the policy rate by the stated margin.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and future earnings pressure, so when unemployment has increased materially over three months, S&P 500 returns over the next month are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with a 0.3pp+ 3-month rise in UNRATE do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect turns positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending, supporting revenue growth and margin stability, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when retail sales momentum is improving.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is negative in more than 60% of rolling 12-month evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while PAYEMS also decelerates, industrial output and labor demand are both fading, implying margin and revenue stress that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth is negative and PAYEMS 3-month change is below zero
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weakness condition does not outperform unconditional returns in predicting negative 60-day SPX outcomes, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: Higher HOUST reflects stronger housing demand and broader cyclical optimism, which supports near-term equity returns as construction activity, financing demand, and consumer sentiment improve together.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing starts break above their 3-month average without producing positive 5-day SPX returns more often than random, or if the effect disappears after controlling for broader market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates that aggregate demand is broadening across both income and spending channels, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month through stronger earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1 and RSAFS strength does not predict above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if either series alone fully captures the signal with no incremental joint effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, falling UNRATE together with rising PAYEMS confirms a genuine labor-market repair, and that combination should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls over 3 months and PAYEMS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the paired labor-repair condition fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than the unconditional sample, or if the effect is absent outside recovery regimes.
